soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed local-limit variance double-counts the kernel scale: s_{mA,n} is already defined as the standard deviation of G_n(h_{m,n}-h_{A,n}), while lambda_min^+(S_n) parameterizes the same covariance scale; either normalize s_{mA,n} or remove the extra lambda factor.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Fix the Conjecture 1 scaling convention by defining s_{mA,n} as a unit-direction loading or by replacing the variance display with a_n^2 s_{mA,n}^2; then restate the iff boundary and §9 window with the same convention.

Give explicit observable formulas, even schematic ones, for h_{D,n}, h_{L,n}, and h_{A,n} from the three named variance estimators so S_n is visibly computable rather than an assumed residual covariance object.

Add a short comparison table for Dorn, Rothe, Armstrong-Kolesar, and Heiler-Kazak with columns for target estimator, overlap regime, studentization object, and why it does not subsume the DML/LRO/ADML frontier.

recommended_next_step: Patch the Conjecture 1 scaling/normalization issue, then rerun the novelty review with the same literature checklist.
